name: Expense_prd

in_scope: All functionality described in the input
out_of_scope: Functionality not described in the input

global_vars.description: Counter for generating unique expense IDs.

data_sources.name: next_id
data_sources.description: Counter for generating unique expense IDs

decomposition_rationale: The parent Expense_prd coordinates the entire expense tracking workflow. It first parses the input (ParseInput), then routes the command to the appropriate handler (RouteCommand). Each handler (AddExpense, ListExpenses, UpdateExpense, DeleteExpense, GetSummary) performs its specific operation using the provided data interfaces and global variables. Finally, FormatOutput returns the result. This decomposition ensures each child has a single responsibility, all parent inputs/outputs are preserved, and dataflow is explicit.

subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and expense_data fields, passed via track_expense function.
  Schema:
    command: string (add|list|update|delete|summary)
    expense_data: {'amount': 'float (required for add/update)', 'category': 'string (required for add/update, one of food/transport/entertainment/shopping/other)', 'description': 'string (optional)', 'date': 'string (optional, YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to today)', 'expense_id': 'integer (required for update/delete)', 'category_filter': 'string (optional for list)', 'start_date': 'string (optional for list/summary, YYYY-MM-DD)', 'end_date': 'string (optional for list/summary, YYYY-MM-DD)', 'group_by': 'string (optional for summary, category|date)'}
  Examples:
    {"command": "add", "expense_data": {"amount": 50.0, "category": "food", "description": "\u5348\u9910"}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string
    data: object (varies by operation)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u8d39\u7528\u8bb0\u5f55\u6dfb\u52a0\u6210\u529f", "data": {"expense_id": 1}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Add Expense: User can add an expense record with amount, category, description, and date. Each record gets a unique ID. Category must be one of: food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other. Amount must be positive. Date defaults to today.
[FR-002] List Expenses: User can list all expense records with optional filtering by category and date range (start_date, end_date). Output shows ID, amount, category, description, date.
[FR-003] Update Expense: User can modify an expense record by ID. Fields that can be updated: amount, category, description. Must check if record exists.
[FR-004] Delete Expense: User can delete an expense record by ID. Must check if record exists.
[FR-005] Get Summary: User can get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range. Returns total amount and amounts per category.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Data stored in memory during session, no persistence.

acceptance_criteria.description: All 5 operations (add, list, update, delete, summary) execute correctly.
Filtering by category and date range works correctly.
Error handling for invalid IDs, negative amounts, invalid categories.

fix_summary.suggestion: If repair_action is redecompose, add/rewrite child boundaries so every child input has a source and parent does not access resources directly.

ParseInput.purpose: Parse the raw input JSON into command and expense_data fields, validating the JSON structure.
ParseInput.behavior: Parses the input JSON and returns command and expense_data. Raises error if input is invalid.
preconditions: Input is a valid JSON string or dict

RouteCommand.purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler based on the command string.
RouteCommand.behavior: Based on command, calls the corresponding child function (AddExpense, ListExpenses, UpdateExpense, DeleteExpense, GetSummary) with expense_data and returns the result.
RouteCommand.signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, expense_data: dict) -> dict
preconditions: command is one of add, list, update, delete, summary
postconditions: result is produced by the appropriate handler

AddExpense.purpose: Add a new expense record after validating input fields.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with amount, category, description, date

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data containing expense_id

AddExpense.behavior: Validates amount is positive, category is one of allowed values, sets default date if missing. Calls expenses.create and next_id.increment via requested_capabilities. Returns success result with new expense_id.
postconditions: New expense record is added to expenses list

ListExpenses.purpose: List expense records with optional filtering by category and date range.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with optional category_filter, start_date, end_date

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data containing list of expenses

ListExpenses.behavior: Calls expenses.list with optional filters. Returns success result with list of expenses.
postconditions: Expenses list is retrieved and filtered

UpdateExpense.purpose: Update an existing expense record by ID.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with expense_id and fields to update

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data

UpdateExpense.behavior: Checks if expense exists via expenses.exists. If exists, calls expenses.update with provided fields. Returns success or error result.
postconditions: Expense record is updated if exists

DeleteExpense.purpose: Delete an expense record by ID.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data

DeleteExpense.behavior: Checks if expense exists via expenses.exists. If exists, calls expenses.delete. Returns success or error result.
postconditions: Expense record is deleted if exists

GetSummary.purpose: Get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with optional start_date, end_date, group_by

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data containing summary

GetSummary.behavior: Calls expenses.list with optional date filters. Then groups expenses by category or date as specified, calculates total and per-group amounts. Returns success result with summary data.

FormatOutput.purpose: Format the result into the standard output JSON structure.
FormatOutput.behavior: Ensures the result dict has the required fields (success, message, data) and returns it as the final output.
preconditions: result is a dict with success, message, data

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent Expense_prd coordinates the entire expense tracking workflow. It first parses the input (ParseInput), then routes the command to the appropriate handler (RouteCommand). Each handler (AddExpense, ListExpenses, UpdateExpense, DeleteExpense, GetSummary) performs its specific operation using the available data interfaces. Finally, the result is formatted (FormatOutput). This decomposition separates concerns: parsing, routing, business logic per operation, and output formatting. All data access is encapsulated in the leaf handlers, which use requested_capabilities to interact with data stores. The parent does not directly access data stores.

ParseInput.purpose: Parse the input JSON to extract command and expense_data.
ParseInput.behavior: Parse the input JSON and return command and expense_data.
preconditions: Input is a valid JSON object with command and expense_data fields

RouteCommand.purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler and return the result.
RouteCommand.behavior: Based on command, call the appropriate child function (AddExpense, ListExpenses, UpdateExpense, DeleteExpense, GetSummary) with expense_data and return the result.
RouteCommand.signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, expense_data: dict) -> dict
preconditions: command is one of add, list, update, delete, summary
postconditions: result contains success, message, and data

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with amount, category, description, date

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data containing expense_id

AddExpense.behavior: Validate amount and category, then call expenses.create to create the record. Return success result with expense_id.
preconditions: category is one of food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other
postconditions: New expense record created with unique ID

ListExpenses.purpose: List expense records with optional filtering.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with optional category_filter, start_date, end_date

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data containing list of expenses

ListExpenses.behavior: Call expenses.list with optional filters and return the list.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with expense_id and fields to update

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data

UpdateExpense.behavior: Check if expense exists using expenses.exists, then call expenses.update. Return success or error.

DeleteExpense.purpose: Delete an expense record by ID.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data

DeleteExpense.behavior: Check if expense exists using expenses.exists, then call expenses.delete. Return success or error.

GetSummary.purpose: Get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with optional start_date, end_date, group_by

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, and data containing summary

GetSummary.behavior: Call expenses.list with optional date filters, then group and calculate totals based on group_by. Return summary.

FormatOutput.purpose: Format the result into the standard output JSON.
FormatOutput.behavior: Return the result as is (already a dict).
preconditions: result is a dict with success, message, data

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent Expense_prd coordinates the entire expense tracking workflow. It first parses the input (ParseInput), then routes the command to the appropriate handler (RouteCommand). Each handler (AddExpense, ListExpenses, UpdateExpense, DeleteExpense, GetSummary) performs its specific operation using the provided data interfaces. Finally, FormatOutput returns the result. The parent directly calls ParseInput, then RouteCommand, then FormatOutput. RouteCommand internally calls the appropriate handler based on command. This ensures all children are used and dataflow is clear.
validation_errors: Conservation violation - Completeness: parent global_var 'expenses' (op: read_write) is not assigned to any child
Conservation violation - Completeness: parent global_var 'next_id' (op: read_write) is not assigned to any child

ParseInput.purpose: Parse the input JSON into command and expense_data fields.
ParseInput.behavior: Parse the input JSON and return command and expense_data fields.

RouteCommand.purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler and return the result.
RouteCommand.behavior: Based on command, call the appropriate child function with expense_data and return its result.
RouteCommand.signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, expense_data: dict) -> dict
postconditions: result is produced by the appropriate handler

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with amount, category, description, date

AddExpense.behavior: Validate amount > 0 and category in allowed list, then call expenses.create and return result.
postconditions: Expense record created with unique ID

ListExpenses.purpose: List expense records with optional filtering.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with optional category_filter, start_date, end_date

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data (list of expenses)

ListExpenses.behavior: Call expenses.list with optional filters and return result.
postconditions: Result contains list of expenses matching filters

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with expense_id and fields to update

UpdateExpense.behavior: Check if expense exists via expenses.exists, then call expenses.update with provided fields. Return error if not found.

DeleteExpense.purpose: Delete an expense record by ID.
DeleteExpense.behavior: Check if expense exists via expenses.exists, then call expenses.delete. Return error if not found.

GetSummary.purpose: Get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range.

inputs.name: expense_data
inputs.description: Expense data with optional start_date, end_date, group_by

GetSummary.behavior: Call expenses.list with optional date filters, then group and sum amounts by specified group_by. Return total and per-group amounts.

FormatOutput.purpose: Format the result into the standard output JSON.
FormatOutput.behavior: Return the result as is (already in JSON format).
